incomplete: 2.2.1 added ONE new trial, unnamed in the changelog and absent from the wiki. See `unknown_2_2_1_trial`.
note: Max 2 trials per run (Crimson x2, +1 via King's Court). Each is a long-lived state flag with a completion condition. `behaviourFlags` are rules that must override normal advice while active.

trials.name: Adrenaline Junkie
trials.requirement: Lose 1 Boon every 25s during interludes until you gain 25 Pulls.
trials.notes: Punishes deliberation — i.e. punishes using this app. Pairs with Stasis.
trials.guideNote: Cross interludes fast to minimise boon loss.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: ends at +30 pulls — pulls close it out

trials.name: Dying Light
trials.requirement: Boons lose 5% Potency every 2.5s, one by one, until you hold Boons totalling 800% Potency.
trials.reward: Rainbow Beacons grant +1 End Reward Sacrifice.
trials.notes: Requires Equilibrium/Orphion's Grace to hit +800%. Guide pairs it with All In for extreme reroll counts.
trials.guideNote: Works extremely well with All In.

trials.name: Monochromokopia
trials.requirement: Until +30 Pulls: completed beacons become Obscured for 7 challenges.
trials.reward: Adds an extra White, Grey and Dark Grey Beacon to the pool.
trials.notes: Reward is excellent (extra White = more challenges, extra Grey = 4th mission). Obscured beacons mean the advisor must reason under uncertainty for 7 challenges.
trials.guideNote: DO NOT TAKE. Completed beacons become unidentifiable.
trials.CORRECTION: I called the reward excellent. Guide rates it C and advises against it — the obscuring cost outweighs the extra White/Grey/DarkGrey.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: adds an extra white/grey/darkGrey to the pool

trials.name: Chronotrigger
trials.requirement: For 12 challenges you cannot gain time in any way.
trials.reward: Green Beacons cleanse 7.5% of Curses and boost Pulls by 1% per curse cleansed (max 5%).
trials.changedIn: 2.2.1 — pull boost cap 3.5% -> 5%. (Changelog also claimed cleanse 15% -> 10%, but the in-game value is 7.5% — changelog wrong or superseded.)
trials.notes: DISABLES THE TIMEOUT GUARD for 12 challenges — the single most dangerous interaction in the game for an advisor to get wrong. Hard-blocks Backup Beat.
trials.guideNote: Time penalties from dying still apply. Green beacons void mob buffs and work from Complete Chaos.
trials.resolvedBy: user, 2026-07-22 — cleanse 7.5% (guide right), pull boost cap 5% (changelog right). Each source had half the truth.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: green cleanses curses and boosts pulls (time gain is void anyway)

trials.name: All In
trials.requirement: For the next 10 challenges curse effects are doubled. Objective: complete 12 challenges.
trials.reward: At run end, convert each End Reward Sacrifice into +3 End Reward Rerolls.
trials.notes: Converts the Redemption/sac axis into rerolls. Wants MANY sacs banked before run end.
trials.guideNote: Provides no sacrifices itself — most effective paired with Dying Light.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: curse effects are DOUBLED — curse beacons are riskier

trials.name: Gambling Beast
trials.requirement: Lose 300 + 90(n-1) seconds on the nth challenge completed. Effects active IMMEDIATELY on taking.
trials.reward: +1 End Reward Reroll per challenge completed
trials.notes: Escalating cost ends the run in ~3 challenges. Correct ONLY as a deliberate final-act cash-out when no good combo formed — exactly the guide's fallback rule.
trials.guideNote: SHOULD ALMOST ALWAYS BE TAKEN AS 2ND TRIAL. Stack Vibrant Aqua/Green beacons to sustain it. Grants no reroll if the timer cannot absorb the cost.
trials.CORRECTION: I rated this extreme/salvage-only. The guide rates it A tier and a near-DEFAULT second trial. My salvage-only framing was wrong.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: drains 300s+ per challenge; time is the binding constraint

trials.name: Hubris
trials.requirement: For 10 challenges, dying ends your Lootrun.
trials.reward: +1 End Reward Reroll and +1 End Reward Sacrifice.
trials.notes: Must produce a persistent UI warning: /kill ends the run instantly.
trials.guideNote: Sleeper pick — easy trial with good rewards.

trials.name: Lights Out
trials.requirement: Until 25 Radiant Challenge Mobs defeated: receive 2 Radiance Chance Curses after every challenge.
trials.reward: +4 Pulls per Radiant Power/Chance curse held, then cleanse them.
trials.notes: Radiance is NEW in 2.2.0. Pairs directly with Radiant Hunter. Under-documented — high value to model well.
trials.guideNote: Avoid multi-mob challenges; use Inner Peace to slow radiance buildup.

trials.name: Side Hustle
trials.requirement: Until 30 chests opened: starting/completing/failing a challenge sets timer to 75s.
trials.notes: SETS the timer (not reduces) — a hard clamp that invalidates all normal time reasoning while active.
trials.guideNote: Timer resets at beacons; dying between challenges costs time; time still gained on red beacons.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: timer clamps until 30 chests opened — chest sources end it

trials.name: Treasury Bill
trials.requirement: Lose 1 Pull every 45s until you obtain 20 end reward pulls. The loss REMOVES trial progress.
trials.notes: Take LATE with a large pull bank — +75% of a big number. Guide: complete via aqua-stacking purples under rainbow.
trials.guideNote: Pair with Lights Out; complete via Purple/Dark Grey beacon stacking.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: bleeds a pull every 45s until +20 pulls

trials.name: Ultimate Sacrifice
trials.requirement: For 10 challenges, your boons are disabled.
trials.notes: Blue Beacons are near-worthless for 10 challenges — advisor must actively downrank blue and boon-generators while active.
trials.guideNote: Recommended as FIRST trial when mob scaling is still low.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: boons are DISABLED for 10 challenges — blue is near-dead

trials.name: Warmth Devourer
trials.requirement: Lose 1 Boon AND 1 Challenge per challenge completed, until you obtain 20 end reward pulls.
trials.reward: +1 End Reward Reroll and +1 End Reward Sacrifice.
trials.notes: Burns 4 challenges per challenge completed. Needs a large challenge bank (post White) or it ends the run.
trials.EXPLOIT: With NO boons held, the trial cannot remove challenges — but still grants its rewards. Effectively free when boon-less.
trials.beaconBiasWhy: eats 3 challenges per challenge until +20 pulls

trials.name: Imperitia
trials.requirement: Until +30 Pulls: spell costs increase by +5 Mana per challenge completed.
trials.notes: Difficulty depends on the player's BUILD, which is outside the state machine. Should be a user-declared preference, not an engine calculation.
trials.guideNote: Never ever take this. Spell cost increase works like Outworn Soldier Gambit; only overridable by the Transcendence major ID.
trials.CORRECTION: I rated this medium risk. Guide rates it unplayable.

trials.name: PLACEHOLDER — 1 unnamed trial added in 2.2.1
trials.notes: Must be collected in-game. With only 2 trial slots per run, one unknown trial out of 14 is a meaningful gap in crimson-beacon advice.

beaconBiasNote: Per-trial beacon steering, applied while the trial is held. Trials impose run-wide constraints so they shift priority hardest — e.g. Ultimate Sacrifice makes blue near-dead, Gambling Beast makes green critical. These SUM with mission and archetype bias, giving combination coverage without enumeration.
